Coffee on the porch with Skeeter this morning, watched the street wake up.
Cut the shelves for Janelle's garage today — oak for honesty.
Walked the raised beds with my coffee; tomatoes and okra coming in.
Building committee meets first Saturday, 8 AM — painting day in November.
Sunday table: collards, cornbread, smothered chicken, peach cobbler.
Called Harold; we'll see each other at the family gathering.
Curtis fished the bayou with me — more about the company than the catch.
Amara and Isaiah helped in the workshop, small tasks and big grins.
Gospel and blues on the workshop radio, Sam Cooke when the mood needs it.
Neighborhood loop walk at 6:30; the knees complain in the damp.
Grilled on the Weber this weekend, cheated on the sodium a little.
CPAP data review with Dr. Pham in November — hate the mask but wear it.
Built a gabled birdhouse, cedar for the smell, gave it away.
Doreen and I bickered about the thermostat, agreed on everything else.
Sun Herald over morning coffee, two cups black by 6.
Home Depot run for saw blades — brand-loyal and slow to buy.
Tithe to Shiloh Baptist; faith the quiet backbone of the week.
Braves on cable tonight, the Saints come Sunday.
Made Mama's gumbo — the weight of home in a pot.
Grandkids over for the weekend, garden and workshop lessons.
Roof's held fine since Zeta, AC newer, the truck runs clean.
Waffle House Saturday, the usual order.
Fixed the porch rocker; the rockers face the street on purpose.
Okra because Mama grew it, herbs by the back door.
